articles.title: Understanding the brain with AI-driven explanations and experiments
articles.summary: Researchers introduce generative causal testing, which translates black box models into clear hypotheses and verifies them in the scanner, revealing what specific brain regions respond to in language.
articles.author: Chandan Singh, Jianfeng Gao

articles.title: Talos: Scaling rare disease diagnosis with automated, iterative genomic reanalysis
articles.summary: Talos was built to help resolve a major bottleneck in genomic medicine: human review time. The open-source system recovered 90% of in-scope diagnoses while surfacing just 1.3 candidate variants per patient for expert review.
articles.author: Jeremiah (Miah) Wander, Cas Simons

articles.title: Ire identifies another LOTUSLITE specimen
articles.summary: Project Ire examined a timely malware sample and determined its intent through reverse engineering—identifying LOTUSLITE characteristics even as most major EDR tools did not detect it.
articles.author: Brian Caswell, Bob Fleck, Mike Walker, Sarah Smith

articles.title: Data Formulator 0.7: AI-powered data analytics for enterprise data
articles.summary: Data Formulator introduces AI-powered analytics for enterprise data workflows. Data teams can easily bring enterprise data into an AI-ready workspace where users can explore, analyze, and visualize data with AI agents to turn raw data into actionable insights.
articles.author: Chenglong Wang, Scott Tsukamaki, Michel Galley, Jianfeng Gao

articles.title: Extending Human Intelligence Through AI
articles.summary: Understanding AI as an extension of human intelligence—not a replacement for it—offers a more grounded path for building trustworthy AI systems.
articles.author: Ken Archer, Harald Wiltsche

articles.title: MagenticLite, MagenticBrain, Fara1.5: An agentic experience optimized for small models
articles.summary: MagenticLite is an agentic system for small models that works across the browser and local file system in a single workflow. It combines specialized models and orchestration to support efficient agentic performance on everyday tasks.
articles.author: Microsoft Research AI Frontiers

articles.title: Vega: Zero-knowledge proofs for digital identity in the age of AI
articles.summary: Vega turns a full credential into a single proof, sharing only what is needed and nothing more, with performance that works in real apps.
articles.author: Srinath Setty

articles.title: Further Notes on Our Recent Research on AI Delegation and Long-Horizon Reliability
articles.summary: Our recent paper, “LLMs Corrupt Your Documents When You Delegate”, has generated discussion about the reliability of AI systems in delegated workflows. We appreciate the interest in this work and want to clarify several important points about what the paper does—and does not—claim. The research aims to develop robust evaluation methods for long-horizon delegated and […]
articles.author: Philippe Laban, Tobias Schnabel, Jennifer Neville

articles.title: mimalloc: A new, high-performance, scalable memory allocator for the modern era
articles.summary: mimalloc is an open-source, modern, scalable memory allocator that is a drop-in replacement for malloc and free. It is relatively small (~12K lines), with clear internal data structures, and is easy to build and integrate into other projects. It provides bounded worst-case allocation times (up to OS primitives), bounded space overhead, low internal fragmentation, and minimal contention by relying almost exclusively on atomic operations.
articles.author: Daan Leijen

articles.title: GridSFM: A new, small foundation model for the electric grid
articles.summary: Introducing GridSFM, a small foundation model that can predict AC optimal power flow in milliseconds, boosting efficiency and unlocking cost savings. Learn how GridSFM gives grid operators direct visibility into congestion, stability, and system health.
articles.author: Weiwei Yang, Andrea Britto Mattos Lima, Thiago Vallin Spina, Spencer Fowers, Baosen   Zhang
